description: Multi-tool AI workflows — verified pipelines people actually use. Auto-discovery via workflow-from-youtube, workflow-from-reddit, workflow-from-github methods in discovery-methods.json.
autoUpdateMethod: Research sweep searches for new workflows monthly. Each workflow tracks its verification status and staleness. Tools within workflows are checked against tools.json — if a tool is deprecated, the workflow gets flagged for update.

workflows.name: AI Short-Form Video Production
workflows.description: End-to-end automated video creation for TikTok/Reels/Shorts
workflows.verifiedSource: n8n template #3121, Like2Byte tutorial, multiple Medium articles
workflows.freeAlternative: Replace Kling with free Runway trial, OpusClip with DaVinci Resolve captions

steps.action: Script generation and scene breakdown
Generate still images per scene (local, free)
Generate voiceover (audio-FIRST — before visuals)
Animate stills with lip-sync (Motion Brush intensity 3-8)
Final edit, captions, export

workflows.orchestration: n8n or Make connects all steps automatically
workflows.keyInsight: Audio is generated BEFORE visuals. Kling Motion Brush intensity 3 for calm, 8 for action. ElevenLabs Stability 45%, Similarity 90%.
gaps: No free video animation tool matches Kling quality yet

connectors.name: AI Video Generator
connectors.note: Pre-built n8n workflow connecting all video pipeline steps

connectors.name: OpusClip
connectors.note: Auto-captioning and platform formatting

workflows.name: Multi-Model AI Coding Pipeline
workflows.description: Use different AI coding tools for what each does best
workflows.verifiedSource: Emergent.sh benchmarks, Builder.io comparison, DEV Community

steps.action: Feature implementation, large refactors, multi-file agentic tasks (80.8% SWE-bench)
Day-to-day editing, iterative refinement, debugging (auto-routes between models)
CI/CD assistance, documentation, inline completions
Adversarial code review in CI (different model reviews the code)

workflows.orchestration: GitHub Actions runs adversarial review step automatically
workflows.keyInsight: Claude Code completed a Next.js build with 33K tokens; Cursor used 188K on the same prompt. They complement, not compete. Agent Teams (Feb 2026) lets Claude Code spawn sub-agents.

connectors.name: Claude Code MCP
connectors.note: Claude Code has built-in MCP support for browser, GitHub, filesystem

connectors.name: GitHub Copilot
connectors.note: VS Code / JetBrains extension

connectors.name: Cursor
connectors.note: Full IDE with built-in multi-model routing

connectors.name: Claude Code Action
connectors.note: Claude reviews PRs in CI/CD

workflows.name: Research & Knowledge Synthesis (3-Model)
workflows.description: Local exploration → structured understanding → polished output

steps.action: Uncapped exploration sandbox — no filters, no rate limits, idea generation
Structured understanding, source grounding, organize research
Final output: interactive prototypes, UI components, documents

workflows.keyInsight: Local models handle discovery (no rate limits), NotebookLM handles verification, Claude handles polished output. Each tool does what it's best at.
gaps: Handoff between tools is manual — could be automated with file watchers

connectors.name: Claude in Chrome
connectors.note: Claude can browse and extract from web pages directly

connectors.name: Google Drive MCP
connectors.note: Claude can read Google Docs for research input

connectors.name: Gmail connector
connectors.note: Claude can read research-related emails

workflows.name: NVIDIA Isaac Sim-to-Real Robotics Pipeline
workflows.description: Train in simulation, deploy on physical robot with zero-shot transfer
workflows.verifiedSource: NVIDIA blog (UR10e gear assembly), ROSCon 2025 multi-robot demo

steps.action: Import URDF, set up environment, add sensors
Synthetic data generation (NVIDIA Cosmos)
RL training with Isaac Lab
Isaac ROS NITROS — GPU-accelerated Visual SLAM, nvBlox, cuMotion, FoundationPose
Edge deployment — software-in-loop then hardware-in-loop

workflows.keyInsight: Zero-shot sim-to-real transfer demonstrated — policy trained entirely in simulation deployed on physical UR10e without fine-tuning.

connectors.name: Isaac ROS NITROS Bridge
connectors.note: GPU-accelerated ROS2 to Isaac Sim connection

connectors.name: isaac_ros_visual_slam
connectors.note: Visual SLAM for localization

connectors.name: nvblox
connectors.note: 3D mapping and obstacle detection

connectors.name: cuMotion
connectors.note: GPU-accelerated motion planning

workflows.name: AI-Assisted PCB Design Pipeline
workflows.description: Text description to manufactured PCB using AI at every step
workflows.freeAlternative: Replace Quilter.ai with KiCad manual routing

steps.action: Parse requirements, cross-reference datasheets, detect design errors (kicad-happy skills)
Schematic capture — KiCad AI Plugin for natural language commands
PCB layout with AI auto-routing via Quilter.ai or manual
3D render of PCB for documentation
Generate Gerbers, BOM, CPL and order fabrication

workflows.orchestration: KiCad MCP Server (52 tools) enables AI-to-KiCad communication
workflows.keyInsight: KiCad's open S-expression file format is what makes AI integration viable. MCP server provides 52 tools and 8 dynamic resources.
gaps: AI auto-routing (Quilter.ai) is paid for complex boards
Schematic → PCB netlist validation still needs human review

connectors.name: KiCAD MCP Server
connectors.note: Claude can control KiCad directly via MCP

connectors.name: kicad-happy
connectors.note: Claude Code skills for KiCad schematic parsing

connectors.name: KiCad AI Plugin (ALT TAB)
connectors.note: Natural language commands in KiCad, supports Claude/GPT/Gemini backends

connectors.name: kicad-jlcpcb-tools
connectors.note: Direct JLCPCB fabrication export from KiCad

connectors.name: pcb2blender
connectors.note: KiCad PCB 3D export to Blender

workflows.name: ElevenLabs Flows — Full-Stack Content
workflows.description: Single-platform multimedia creation: video + audio + effects via visual canvas
workflows.verifiedSource: ElevenLabs official launch March 2026, Feisworld guide
workflows.freeAlternative: Use separate free tools: Stable Diffusion + Coqui TTS + DaVinci Resolve

steps.action: Visual node-based canvas: connect text → image/video → audio → output
steps.paidNote: Free tier limited, full Flows requires subscription

steps.action: 35+ integrated models: Veo, Sora, Kling, Wan, Flux, Seedance for visuals
TTS, voice cloning, lip-sync, SFX, music generation
Non-destructive iteration: change one node, only downstream re-renders

workflows.orchestration: Built-in — ElevenLabs Flows IS the orchestrator
workflows.keyInsight: Non-destructive iteration means changing one node only re-processes downstream. Consolidates 3-5 separate tools into one platform. This is the consolidation trend in action.
gaps: Vendor lock-in risk — everything depends on ElevenLabs
Free tier may be too limited

workflows.name: Self-Hosted Private AI Stack
workflows.description: Fully private, uncapped AI assistant running locally

steps.action: Local model runtime — download and run open models
Alternative: GUI-based local model management
Open WebUI: ChatGPT-like interface with RAG, multi-user, function calling

workflows.orchestration: Open WebUI handles everything after Ollama is running
workflows.keyInsight: Mini PC (~$800 one-time) replaces ~$200/month in cloud API costs. No rate limits, no data leaving the device. ROI in 4 months.
gaps: Model quality still behind closed models for complex tasks

connectors.name: Open WebUI
connectors.note: ChatGPT-like interface for Ollama with RAG, plugins, function calling

connectors.name: Ollama API
connectors.note: OpenAI-compatible API for local models

workflows.name: Content Creation Pipeline
workflows.description: Blog posts, articles, social media content using AI writing + images + editing
workflows.verifiedSource: Original project workflow — not independently verified

steps.action: Draft content with outline and key points
Refine tone, check accuracy, improve structure
Generate images locally (free, no credits)
Final grammar, clarity, style polish

workflows.keyInsight: Use different AI for drafting vs refining — each has different strengths.
gaps: Midjourney produces better images but costs $14+ CAD/month

metaInsights.orchestrationTrend: The competitive advantage in 2026 is not which AI tool you use but how you connect them. n8n and Make appear repeatedly as the glue layer. MCP (Model Context Protocol) is emerging as the standardized integration protocol.
metaInsights.consolidationTrend: ElevenLabs Flows represents platforms absorbing the orchestration layer. Watch for this pattern — it reduces flexibility but increases ease of use.
metaInsights.costRule: 8-hour rule: if a tool doesn't save at least 8 hours per $50/month, cut it from the stack.
metaInsights.freeFirstPrinciple: Every workflow should have a fully free path. Paid upgrades are optional improvements, not requirements.
